display_name: Consumer Complaint (Section 35, Consumer Protection Act, 2019) before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission
structural_summary: A pleading filed before a District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. Layout: forum heading naming the Commission and district; complaint number placeholder (Consumer Complaint No. ___ of 20__); cause title giving the complainant's name, address and occupation versus the opposite party with its registered/branch address; title line "Complaint under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019"; numbered paragraphs covering parties, the transaction with dates and amounts, defect/deficiency/unfair-trade-practice averments, correspondence and demands, territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction paragraphs, and a limitation paragraph; prayer clause listing reliefs (refund, replacement, compensation, costs); place and date; signatures of complainant and advocate; verification clause; supporting affidavit block; list of documents annexed.
statutory_requirements:
- Title the complaint as filed under Section 35 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission of the relevant district.
- Plead that the complainant is a "consumer" within the meaning of Section 2(7), having bought goods or hired services for consideration and not for resale or a commercial purpose.
- Aver deficiency in service as defined in Section 2(11), and/or unfair trade practice or defect in the goods [VERIFY: Section 2(10) for "defect"; Section 2(47) for "unfair trade practice"].
- Plead territorial jurisdiction: opposite party's office/branch, place where the cause of action arose, or the complainant's residence [VERIFY: Section 34(2)(d) for the complainant-residence option].
- Plead pecuniary jurisdiction stating the value of the goods or services paid as consideration falls within the District Commission's limits [VERIFY: current pecuniary limits — revised by Central Government notification; do not assert figures].
- Limitation paragraph: complaint is filed within two years of the date on which the cause of action arose, per Section 69; plead the dates establishing this.
- Prayer: refund or replacement, removal of the deficiency, compensation for loss, mental agony and harassment, and costs of litigation [VERIFY: Section 39 as the provision enumerating reliefs].
- Verification by the complainant with place and date, stating the contents are true and correct.
- Supporting affidavit of the complainant; annex invoices and correspondence with a list of documents.
- Pay the prescribed fee [VERIFY: fee schedule under the Consumer Protection (Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions) Rules, 2020].
